title: Add --enable-webhooks CLI flag for webhook gating
description:
**Size:** 🔧 small
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** critical
**Source:** GitHub Issue [#1](https://github.com/C5C3/memcached-operator/issues/1)

Add an `--enable-webhooks` boolean CLI flag (default `true`) to `cmd/main.go` that conditionally creates the webhook server and registers admission webhooks. When `false`, skip `webhook.NewServer()` creation (lines 104-106), pass nil `WebhookServer` to `ctrl.NewManager`, skip `SetupMemcachedWebhookWithManager()` call (lines 141-144), and log an info message. Update `charts/memcached-operator/templates/deployment.yaml` (lines 36-45) to pass `--enable-webhooks=false` when `.Values.webhook.enabled` is false. Add table-driven unit test in `cmd/main_test.go` following the existing `TestParseWatchNamespaces` pattern (lines 10-98).

**Rationale:** The operator pod enters CrashLoopBackOff when deployed with `webhook.enabled: false` because the binary unconditionally starts the webhook server and tries to load TLS certs from a volume that isn't mounted. This blocks webhook-less deployments needed for kind clusters and CI environments where cert-manager TLS provisioning is problematic. Observed in production CI with `context deadline exceeded` on Helm install.

**Affected Areas:**
- cmd/main.go
- cmd/main_test.go
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/deployment.yaml

stories.title: Operator starts without webhooks in environments lacking cert-manager
stories.want: to deploy the memcached-operator with webhooks disabled so the pod does not CrashLoopBackOff when TLS certs are unavailable
stories.so_that: I can run the operator in kind clusters and CI environments where cert-manager TLS provisioning is problematic
criteria: Passing --enable-webhooks=false skips webhook.NewServer() creation and passes nil WebhookServer to ctrl.NewManager
An info-level log message 'webhooks are disabled' is emitted when --enable-webhooks=false
The operator starts successfully and passes health/readiness probes without TLS certs mounted

stories.title: Operator starts with webhooks enabled by default
stories.want: the operator to create the webhook server and register admission webhooks when --enable-webhooks is not explicitly set
stories.so_that: existing deployments are not broken by the new flag and webhook behavior remains the default
criteria: When --enable-webhooks is omitted, the flag defaults to true
When --enable-webhooks=true (or omitted), webhook.NewServer() is created with TLS opts and passed to ctrl.NewManager
No behavioral change from the current code when the flag is not provided

stories.title: Helm chart passes --enable-webhooks=false when webhook.enabled is false
stories.want: the Helm chart deployment template to automatically pass --enable-webhooks=false to the operator binary when I set webhook.enabled: false in values.yaml
stories.so_that: I don't have to manually configure CLI args and the Helm value consistently controls all webhook-related resources
criteria: When .Values.webhook.enabled is false, --enable-webhooks=false appears in the container args
When .Values.webhook.enabled is true (default), --enable-webhooks=false does NOT appear in the container args
The arg is placed in the existing args block alongside other conditional args (leader-elect, watch-namespaces)

requirements.description: The operator binary SHALL accept an --enable-webhooks boolean CLI flag that defaults to true
requirements.rationale: Backward compatibility requires the default to be true so existing deployments are unaffected

scenarios.name: Flag defaults to true when omitted
scenarios.when: the operator binary is started without --enable-webhooks
and_then: webhook server is created and webhooks are registered

scenarios.name: Flag explicitly set to true
scenarios.when: the operator binary is started with --enable-webhooks=true
and_then: webhook server is created and webhooks are registered

scenarios.name: Flag explicitly set to false
scenarios.when: the operator binary is started with --enable-webhooks=false
and_then: webhook server is NOT created and webhooks are NOT registered

requirements.description: When --enable-webhooks is false, the operator SHALL skip webhook.NewServer() creation and pass nil WebhookServer to ctrl.NewManager
requirements.rationale: The webhook server attempts to load TLS certs on startup; skipping it prevents CrashLoopBackOff when certs are not mounted

scenarios.name: Webhook server skipped when disabled
scenarios.then: webhook.NewServer() is not called and WebhookServer field in ctrl.Options is nil
and_then: the manager starts without a webhook listener on port 9443

scenarios.name: Webhook server created when enabled
scenarios.then: webhook.NewServer() is called with the configured TLS opts and passed as WebhookServer to ctrl.NewManager
and_then: the manager starts with webhook listener on port 9443

requirements.description: When --enable-webhooks is false, the operator SHALL skip the SetupMemcachedWebhookWithManager() call
requirements.rationale: Registering webhooks without a webhook server would cause a runtime panic or error

scenarios.name: Webhook registration skipped when disabled
and_then: no mutating/validating webhook handlers are registered with the manager

scenarios.name: Webhook registration performed when enabled
and_then: mutating and validating webhook handlers are registered

requirements.description: When --enable-webhooks is false, the operator SHALL log an info-level message indicating webhooks are disabled
requirements.rationale: Operators need observability into why webhooks are not running for debugging and audit purposes

scenarios.name: Info log emitted when webhooks disabled
scenarios.then: an info-level log message containing 'webhooks are disabled' is emitted via setupLog

scenarios.name: No disabled message when webhooks enabled
scenarios.then: no 'webhooks are disabled' log message is emitted

requirements.description: The Helm deployment template SHALL pass --enable-webhooks=false to the container args when .Values.webhook.enabled is false
requirements.rationale: The Helm chart already gates webhook resources (ports, volumes, cert-manager CRs) on webhook.enabled; the CLI flag must be consistent

scenarios.name: Flag injected when webhooks disabled in Helm
scenarios.then: --enable-webhooks=false appears in the container args list in the rendered Deployment
and_then: the arg is placed in the args block between other conditional args

scenarios.name: Flag not injected when webhooks enabled in Helm
scenarios.then: --enable-webhooks=false does NOT appear in the rendered container args
and_then: the operator starts with webhooks enabled by default

tasks.title: Add --enable-webhooks flag and conditional webhook logic in cmd/main.go (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004)
tasks.description: In cmd/main.go: (1) Add `var enableWebhooks bool` alongside existing flag vars at line ~68. (2) Add `flag.BoolVar(&enableWebhooks, "enable-webhooks", true, "Enable webhook server and admission webhook registration.")` alongside existing flag registrations at line ~76. (3) Wrap lines 104-106 (webhook.NewServer) in `if enableWebhooks { ... }` and declare `var webhookServer *webhook.Server` before the conditional so it remains nil when false. (4) Wrap lines 141-144 (SetupMemcachedWebhookWithManager) in `if enableWebhooks { ... }`. (5) Add `else { setupLog.Info("webhooks are disabled") }` to the webhook server conditional block. (6) Change line 121 to use the conditional webhookServer variable instead of the direct call result.

tasks.title: Add table-driven unit test for --enable-webhooks flag parsing in cmd/main_test.go (REQ-001)
tasks.description: In cmd/main_test.go: Add a new exported function (e.g., `buildWebhookServer`) in main.go that takes enableWebhooks bool and tlsOpts, returns *webhook.Server (nil when false, real server when true). Then add TestBuildWebhookServer table-driven test following the exact TestParseWatchNamespaces pattern: test cases for (1) enableWebhooks=true returns non-nil *webhook.Server, (2) enableWebhooks=false returns nil. Use reflect.DeepEqual or simple nil checks.

tasks.title: Update Helm deployment.yaml to pass --enable-webhooks=false when webhook.enabled is false (REQ-005)
tasks.description: In charts/memcached-operator/templates/deployment.yaml: Add a new conditional arg block in the args section (after the watch-namespaces block at line 45, before the image line at line 46): `{{- if not .Values.webhook.enabled }}\n            - --enable-webhooks=false\n            {{- end }}`. This follows the existing pattern used by leaderElection.enabled and watchNamespaces conditionals.

tasks.title: Add Helm template render test for --enable-webhooks flag injection (REQ-005)
tasks.description: Verify the Helm template renders correctly by running `helm template` with webhook.enabled=false and webhook.enabled=true, then checking the args output. If the project has existing Helm template tests (e.g., in charts/memcached-operator/tests/), add test cases there. Otherwise, document the manual verification commands. Test cases: (1) default values (webhook.enabled=true) — --enable-webhooks=false must NOT be in args, (2) webhook.enabled=false — --enable-webhooks=false must be in args.

test_specifications.story: Operator starts with webhooks enabled by default
test_specifications.expected: When enableWebhooks is true, buildWebhookServer returns a non-nil *webhook.Server

test_specifications.story: Operator starts without webhooks in environments lacking cert-manager
test_specifications.expected: When enableWebhooks is false, buildWebhookServer returns nil

test_specifications.story: Helm chart passes --enable-webhooks=false when webhook.enabled is false
test_specifications.expected: helm template with default values does NOT include --enable-webhooks=false in container args

test_specifications.story: Helm chart passes --enable-webhooks=false when webhook.enabled is false
test_specifications.expected: helm template with --set webhook.enabled=false includes --enable-webhooks=false in container args

review_criteria: The --enable-webhooks flag defaults to true, preserving backward compatibility with existing deployments
When --enable-webhooks=false, no webhook.NewServer() is called and WebhookServer is nil in ctrl.Options
An info-level log message is emitted when webhooks are disabled
Helm template renders --enable-webhooks=false in args when .Values.webhook.enabled is false and omits it when true
Table-driven unit test in cmd/main_test.go follows the existing TestParseWatchNamespaces pattern
golangci-lint passes without errors on modified Go files
go vet passes without errors on modified Go files
implementation_notes:
Architecture: Extract webhook server construction into a testable function `buildWebhookServer(enableWebhooks bool, tlsOpts []func(*tls.Config)) *webhook.Server` that returns nil when disabled and a configured server when enabled. This follows the same pattern as `parseWatchNamespaces` — a pure function extracted from main() for testability.

Key files to modify:
- `cmd/main.go:62-160` — Add flag var, flag registration, conditional webhook logic, extract buildWebhookServer
- `cmd/main_test.go:1-98` — Add TestBuildWebhookServer table-driven test following TestParseWatchNamespaces pattern
- `charts/memcached-operator/templates/deployment.yaml:36-45` — Add conditional --enable-webhooks=false arg

Pitfalls:
- The webhookServer variable must be declared as `var webhookServer *webhook.Server` (pointer type) so it defaults to nil when webhooks are disabled. controller-runtime's ctrl.Options.WebhookServer accepts the webhook.Server interface, and nil is a valid value that disables the webhook listener.
- The log message must use setupLog.Info() to be consistent with existing log messages in main.go.
- The Helm template conditional must use `{{- if not .Values.webhook.enabled }}` (not `{{- if eq .Values.webhook.enabled false }}`) to match the existing negation pattern used elsewhere in the chart.
